subtopics.name: Cellular Energy & ATP Features
learning_outcomes: Outline the need for energy in living organisms, as illustrated by active transport, movement and anabolic reactions [cite: 12]
Describe the features of ATP that make it suitable as the universal energy currency [cite: 13]
State that ATP is synthesised by transfer of phosphate in substrate-linked reactions and chemiosmosis [cite: 15, 16]
Explain the relative energy values of carbohydrates, lipids and proteins as respiratory substrates [cite: 17]
State the definition and calculation of Respiratory Quotient (RQ) [cite: 18, 19]
Describe and carry out investigations using simple respirometers to determine the RQ of organisms [cite: 20, 21]

subtopics.name: Stages and Pathways of Respiration
learning_outcomes: State the locations of glycolysis, link reaction, Krebs cycle, and oxidative phosphorylation [cite: 27, 28, 29, 30, 31]
Outline glycolysis as the phosphorylation of glucose and splitting of fructose 1,6-bisphosphate into triose phosphate and pyruvate [cite: 32]
Describe the link reaction and the role of coenzyme A [cite: 33, 34]
Outline the Krebs cycle involving oxaloacetate and citrate [cite: 35, 36, 37]
Describe the role of NAD and FAD in hydrogen transfer [cite: 38]
Explain the process of oxidative phosphorylation, the electron transport chain, and ATP synthesis [cite: 39, 40, 41, 42, 43]
Relate mitochondrial structure to function using diagrams and micrographs [cite: 44]
Outline anaerobic respiration (lactate and ethanol fermentation) and compare ATP yields to aerobic conditions [cite: 44, 51]
Explain rice adaptations (aerenchyma and ethanol fermentation) for submerged growth [cite: 51]
Investigate respiration rates using redox indicators and respirometers [cite: 51]

subtopics.name: Photosynthesis: Light & Calvin Cycle
learning_outcomes: Relate chloroplast structure to its function in energy transfer [cite: 65]
Explain the use of ATP and reduced NADP from the light-dependent stage in the Calvin cycle [cite: 66]
Identify the thylakoids and stroma as the sites of specific photosynthetic stages [cite: 67]
Describe the roles of pigments (chlorophylls, carotene, xanthophyll) and interpret absorption/action spectra [cite: 68, 69]
Use chromatography to separate and identify chloroplast pigments using Rf values [cite: 70]
Explain cyclic and non-cyclic photophosphorylation and the synthesis of ATP/reduced NADP [cite: 71, 72, 73, 76, 81]
Describe the role of the electron transport chain and chemiosmosis in the thylakoid membrane [cite: 89, 92, 93]
Outline the three stages of the Calvin cycle: carbon fixation, reduction of GP, and regeneration of RuBP [cite: 89]
State how Calvin cycle intermediates produce carbohydrates, lipids, and amino acids [cite: 89]

subtopics.name: Photosynthesis: Limiting Factors
learning_outcomes: State that light intensity, CO2 concentration, and temperature are limiting factors [cite: 89]
Explain the effects of changes in these limiting factors on the rate of photosynthesis [cite: 89]
Investigate light intensity and wavelength using redox indicators and chloroplast suspensions [cite: 89]
Investigate environmental effects on whole aquatic or terrestrial plants [cite: 89]

subtopics.name: Mammalian Homeostasis & Kidneys
learning_outcomes: Explain the principles of homeostasis, including negative feedback, receptors, and effectors [cite: 105, 106]
Describe kidney structure and the parts of a nephron [cite: 108, 117]
Explain urine formation via ultrafiltration and selective reabsorption [cite: 124, 125, 126, 127]
Describe osmoregulation involving the hypothalamus, ADH, and aquaporins [cite: 128]
Explain blood glucose control via cell signalling, insulin, and glucagon [cite: 134]
Explain the operation of glucose test strips and biosensors [cite: 134]

subtopics.name: Plant Homeostasis & Stomata
learning_outcomes: Explain how stomatal aperture balances CO2 uptake and transpiration [cite: 134]
Describe the mechanism of guard cell action and the role of abscisic acid during water stress [cite: 134]

subtopics.name: Mammalian Nervous & Muscular Control
learning_outcomes: Compare the features of the endocrine and nervous systems [cite: 145, 146]
Describe neurone structures and the sequence of events leading to an action potential [cite: 147, 149]
Explain changes in membrane potential, saltatory conduction, and the refractory period [cite: 150, 154, 155]
Describe the structure and function of a cholinergic synapse [cite: 156]
Describe the stimulation and ultrastructure of striated muscle [cite: 157, 158]
Explain the sliding filament model of muscular contraction [cite: 159]

subtopics.name: Plant Growth & Coordination
learning_outcomes: Describe the rapid response and closure mechanism of the Venus fly trap [cite: 163]
Explain the role of auxin in elongation growth and cell wall acidification [cite: 163]
Describe the role of gibberellin in barley germination [cite: 163]

subtopics.name: Inheritance: Meiosis & Variation
learning_outcomes: Explain haploid and diploid terms and the need for reduction division [cite: 173]
Describe the behaviour of chromosomes during the main stages of meiosis [cite: 173]
Explain how crossing over and independent assortment produce genetic variation [cite: 173]

subtopics.name: Inheritance: Genetic Crosses & Phenotypes
learning_outcomes: Define key genetic terms (allele, dominant, linkage, epistasis, etc.) [cite: 173]
Construct genetic diagrams for monohybrid and dihybrid crosses [cite: 173]
Apply the chi-squared test to determine the significance of results [cite: 173]
Explain the relationship between specific genes (TYR, HBB, F8, HTT) and disease phenotypes [cite: 180]

subtopics.name: Inheritance: Gene Control Mechanisms
learning_outcomes: Distinguish between structural/regulatory genes and repressible/inducible enzymes [cite: 180]
Explain the lac operon in prokaryotes and transcription factors in eukaryotes [cite: 180]
Explain how gibberellin activates genes by breaking down DELLA proteins [cite: 180]

subtopics.name: Evolution: Types of Variation
learning_outcomes: Explain the genetic and environmental basis of continuous and discontinuous variation [cite: 191]
Use the t-test to compare means of different samples [cite: 191]

subtopics.name: Evolution: Selection & Breeding
learning_outcomes: Explain natural selection as a 'struggle for existence' leading to survival of the best adapted [cite: 191]
Describe stabilising, disruptive, and directional selection, and genetic drift [cite: 191]
Apply the Hardy-Weinberg principle to calculate allele frequencies [cite: 191]
Outline principles and examples of selective breeding (artificial selection) [cite: 191]

subtopics.name: Evolution: Speciation & DNA
learning_outcomes: Outline evolution as a process of gene pool changes over time [cite: 196]
Discuss the use of DNA sequence data to show evolutionary relationships [cite: 196]
Explain allopatric and sympatric speciation via genetic isolation [cite: 196]

subtopics.name: Biodiversity: Classification Systems
learning_outcomes: Discuss biological, morphological, and ecological species concepts [cite: 211]
Describe the three domains (Archaea, Bacteria, Eukarya) and the taxonomic hierarchy [cite: 212, 216]
Outline characteristic features of the five kingdoms and the classification of viruses [cite: 217, 218]

subtopics.name: Biodiversity: Fieldwork & Indices
learning_outcomes: Define ecosystem and niche, and explain levels of biodiversity assessment [cite: 221, 222]
Describe field methods (quadrats, transects, mark-release-recapture) [cite: 227]
Use Spearman’s rank, Pearson’s correlation, and Simpson’s index of diversity [cite: 233]

subtopics.name: Biodiversity: Conservation Strategies
learning_outcomes: Explain causes of extinction and reasons for maintaining biodiversity [cite: 233]
Outline the roles of zoos, botanic gardens, seed banks, and assisted reproduction [cite: 233]
Describe the roles of IUCN, CITES, and the control of invasive alien species [cite: 233]

subtopics.name: Genetic Tech: Tools & Principles
learning_outcomes: Define recombinant DNA and explain the roles of restriction enzymes, ligase, and vectors [cite: 241]
Explain the use of promoters, marker genes, and gene editing [cite: 241]
Describe PCR, gel electrophoresis, microarrays, and the use of genetic databases [cite: 241]

subtopics.name: Genetic Tech: Medical Applications
learning_outcomes: Explain the advantages of recombinant proteins, genetic screening, and gene therapy [cite: 248]
Discuss social and ethical considerations of genetic technology in medicine [cite: 248]

subtopics.name: Genetic Tech: GMOs in Agriculture
learning_outcomes: Explain how genetic engineering improves crop productivity and quality (e.g., GM salmon, soybean) [cite: 248]
Discuss ethical and social implications of GMOs in food production [cite: 248]
